data.title: Legrand SA 2025 Q2 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation
data.description: The following slide deck was published by Legrand SA in conjunction with their 2025 Q2 earnings call.

data.title: Nickel Miners News For The Month Of July 2025
data.description:
Welcome to the nickel miners news for July.

The past month saw nickel prices flat and LME inventory slightly higher.

Nickel price news

As of July 31, the nickel spot price was USD 15,085/t, roughly flat from USD 15,105/tonne last month.

Nickel inventory at the London Metals Exchange [LME] was slightly higher the past month at 204,912 tonnes (204,360 tonnes last month).

Nickel spot price 10 year chart - Current price = USD 15,085/t (source)

Nickel demand v supply chart

S&P Global Market Intelligence forecast nickel oversupply to 2027, then a 'primary' nickel deficit starting in 2028 (as of June 2024) (source)

Carbon Credits forecasts a nickel oversupply to 2030, then growing deficits from 2030-2035 (as of May 2025) (source)

Trend Investing v IEA demand forecast for EV metals (IEA)

2022 - IEA forecasts 60 new nickel mines needed by 2030

Lithium & Nickel are the two 'high importance to energy' critical materials in the medium term (2025-2035) (source - page 29)

Nickel Market News

A relevant March 7 article reported:

Canada may redirect nickel exports as US tariffs escalate trade tensions, say Nickel CEO. Canada Nickel Company CEO Mark Selby says Canada supplies half of US nickel but can find new buyers if trade tensions escalate.

On July 2 Discovery Alert reported:

2025 nickel sulphate prices: Key market drivers and outlook...Several nickel salt smelters have implemented strategic maintenance shutdowns in response to profitability challenges...Regional production concentration remains heavily weighted toward Indonesia and China, with the latter accounting for approximately 65% of global refined nickel sulphate output...The supply side currently demonstrates limited elasticity in response to price signals, with producers preferring operational adjustments over price concessions—a dynamic that supports the current price floor despite demand weakness...The demand side of the nickel sulphate equation shows notable weakness, creating the fundamental imbalance currently characterizing the market...LFP (lithium iron phosphate): Gaining ground in entry-level segments, containing no nickel...The prevailing market sentiment suggests price stability will continue in the near term, with the 27,000-28,000 yuan/mt range likely to persist through Q3 2025 unless significant supply or demand disruptions materialize.

On July 7 Discovery Alert reported:

How US tariff policies are influencing global nickel prices in 2025...The global nickel market is experiencing significant turbulence as the United States prepares to implement new tariff policies...US tariffs on various metals and goods, including nickel, are scheduled to take effect on August 1, 2025...Major nickel producers Indonesia and Russia could face significant tariff impacts, potentially reshaping global supply chains...This trade diversion could create regional price disparities, with potential discounts in Asian markets and premiums in US markets if supply tightens...Unlike aluminum or steel, which have significant domestic US production capacity, nickel relies heavily on imports with limited domestic production alternatives. This supply dynamic makes nickel particularly vulnerable to tariff effects.

Note: An April 4 article by Fastmarkets reported: "Critical minerals sidestep US ‘Liberation Day’ reciprocal tariffs. Critical minerals appear to be exempt from the 'reciprocal' tariffs introduced by Donald Trump on Wednesday April 2; US trade paralyzed by confusion, market participants reported." Nickel is classified by the USA as a critical mineral.

In July Fastmarkets reported:

Nickel: Stagnant nickel prices amid oversupply...Fundamentally, this oversupply is due more to strong production growth rather than to weak demand. Without greater supply discipline from Indonesia and China, which together account for 78% of global refined production, there is little scope for any near-term market rebalancing and thus for any sustained price recovery.

Nickel Company News

Producers

Norilsk Nickel ("Nornickel")

On July 21 Nornickel announced:

Nornickel announces consolidated production results for 1H 2025...In 1H 2025, the output of all key metals decreased compared to the same period of last year...In 1H 2025, consolidated nickel output decreased 4% year-on-year (y-o-y) to 87 kt.

Note: Nornickel nickel production guidance for 2025 is 196-204kt.

Vale SA (NYSE:VALE)

Vale Voisey's Bay Mine is a key Canadian and global source of nickel. Vale plans a nickel sulphate refinery at Bécancour in Québec to supply GM with battery grade nickel sulfate with deliveries targeted to commence in H2 2026, subject to various regulatory approvals.

On July 22 Vale announced:

Vale's production and sales in 2Q25. Vale's second quarter was marked by solid performance across all business segments...Sustained operational improvements at both Nickel and Copper drove record Q2 output since 2021 and 2019, respectively...Nickel production totaled 40.3 kt, 44% (12.4 kt) higher y/y, driven by stronger asset performance in Canada and Onça Puma and lower planned maintenance activity, further fueled by VBME’s ramp-up.

Glencore [HK:805] [LSE:GLEN] (OTCPK:GLCNF)

On July 7, Glencore announced:

2025/2026 share buy-back programme. Glencore plc (the "Company") announces the commencement of a programme to make market purchases of its ordinary shares (the "Shares") of an aggregate value of up to USD 1 billion (the "Programme"), subject to market conditions, with completion planned to be by the time of the release of our financial results for the 2025 year in February 2026.

On July 30 Glencore announced:

Half-Year Production Report 2025...Adjusting for 5,000 tonnes of Koniambo production in the base period (prior to its transition to care and maintenance), own sourced nickel production of 36,600 tonnes was 2,600 tonnes (7%) lower than H1 2024, due to Murrin Murrin maintenance downtime.

Note: The un-adjusted result is 17% lower nickel production in H1, 2025. Nickel guidance for 2025 is 74-80kt.

BHP Group [ASX:BHP] (NYSE:BHP)

BHP's Nickel West operations include the Mt Keith Nickel Mine in Australia. Nickel West produced 81.6 kt of nickel in the '24 financial year. In 2024 BHP temporarily suspended Western Australia Nickel operations due to low nickel prices. Stage 1 production of the Kwinana Nickel Refinery is aimed to be 100ktpa nickel sulphate.

On July 14 BHP Group announced: "BHP explores opportunities with BYD in battery electrification."

On July 18 BHP announced:

BHP Operational Review for the year ended 30 June 2025. Record copper and iron ore production drives strong 2025 performance...

Note: Nickel production in Q4, FY 2025 was 0.3kt, down 99% YoY, as Western Australia Nickel transitioned into temporary suspension in HY25. "BHP is assessing the potential divestment of the WAN assets."

Anglo American [LSX:AAL] (OTCPK:AAUKF)

Anglo American's sale of their nickel business to MMG Singapore Resources continue to target completion by Q3 2025.

On July 24 Anglo American announced:

Q2 2025 Production Report...another solid quarter in Copper and Iron Ore, with both businesses tracking to guidance... we are continuing to progress the nickel and steelmaking coal transactions. (Q2) Nickel production decreased by 5% to 9,500 tonnes, reflecting expected lower grade.

Eramet [FRA:ERA] (OTCPK:ERMAY)

On July 30 Eramet announced:

Eramet: Increased focus on operational efficiency following a highly pressured H1 2025...Adjusted EBITDA (excluding SLN)2 at €191m, down 45% vs. H1 2024, primarily from the re-duced contribution of PT WBN (-€92m, representing nearly 2/3 of the decline) attributable to:

  • The planned start of new mining production sites at Weda Bay in Indonesia, compounded by constrained operating permit, which led to a significant decline in nickel grades (from 2.0% to 1.6% for saprolite), along with increased in operating costs.
  • Unfavourable product mix impacting on volumes sold (-8%)...

Net Income, Group share (excluding SLN)2 negative at -€101m.

Sherritt International (OTCPK:SHERF)[TSX:S]

On July 29 Sherritt International announced:

Sherritt Reports Second Quarter 2025 results and updated guidance; Moa JV expansion Phase Two ramp up commencing; Further cost reductions... Finished nickel and cobalt production at the Moa Joint Venture (“Moa JV”) was 3,431 tonnes and 389 tonnes, respectively (Sherritt’s share(1))...Net direct cash cost (“NDCC”)(3) was US$5.27/lb benefiting from higher cobalt, fertilizer and other by-product credits and lower maintenance cost...

  • Net earnings from continuing operations was $10.4 million, or $0.02 per share.
  • Adjusted net loss from continuing operations(3) was $25.6 million or $(0.06) per share which primarily excludes $32.4 million gain on Sherritt’s debt and equity transactions completed in the quarter.

IGO Limited [ASX:IGO] (OTC:IIDDY)

IGO state on their website: "In June 2024, Cosmos was transitioned into care and maintenance. IGO intends to protect the optionality to restart Cosmos in the future." Forrestania to enter care & maintenance before end CY24. The Nova Mine is their flagship and low cost nickel mine with limited mine life remaining. IGO also has large lithium JV assets.

On July 30 IGO announced:

Quarterly Report Period ended 30 June 2025...Higher quarterly production and lower unit cash costs at Nova, completing a strong second half to FY25...IGO underlying EBITDA of $62M for 4Q25 (3Q25: $34M)...Nova life of mine production guidance maintained at 15,000-18,000t of contained nickel.

Note: FYQ4 2025 nickel production was 5.107kt, up 22% QoQ.

Jinchuan Group (owns ~60% of Jinchuan Group International Resources [HKEX:2362])

On June 30 Market screener reported: "Jinchuan Group International Resources Co. Ltd Announces Change of Composition of the Board Committees, Effective 30 June 2025."

Sumitomo Metal Mining Co. (OTCPK:SMMYY) ("SMM")

On July 1 Sumitomo Metal Mining announced:

Notice regarding the status of repurchase of own shares...Total number of shares repurchased 1,047,000 shares. 3. Total amount of repurchase price 3,501,183,800 yen 4. Repurchase period From June 1, 2025 to June 30, 2025.

On July 2 Market Screener reported:

Tranche Update on Sumitomo Metal Mining Co., Ltd.'s Equity Buyback Plan announced on May 12, 2025. From May 12, 2025 to June 30, 2025, the company has repurchased 2,594,400 shares, representing 0.94% for ¥8,501.18 million. With this, the company has completed the repurchase of 2,594,400 shares, representing 0.94% for ¥8,501.18 million under the buyback announced on May 12, 2025.

Nickel 28 Capital Corp. [TSXV:NKL] [GR:3JC]

Nickel 28 holds an 8.56% JV interest in Ramu which is operated by the Metallurgical Corporation of China (“MCC”).

No news for the month.

Nickel Industries Limited [ASX:NIC] (OTCPK:NICMF)

On July 30 Nickel Industries announced:

QUARTERLY ACTIVITIES REPORT for the quarter ended 30 June 2025...Record quarterly Hengjaya Mine ore sales of 3,021,678 tonnes and EBITDA of US$41.4m. Integrated refinery ready for commissioning.

Note: RKEF operations (80% share) Q2 nickel production was 30.463kt, down 4% QoQ.

Other nickel producers

Tsingshan Holding Group Co Ltd (private, China's largest nickel producer, Chinese owned stainless steel producer with large nickel mines in Indonesia), First Quantum Minerals [TSX:FM] (OTCPK:FQVLF), Franco/Nevada [TSX:FNV], MMG [HK:1208], South32 [ASX:S32], Lundin Mining [TSX:LUN], Nickel Asia Corporation [PSE:NIKL], Pacific Nickel Mines [ASX:PNM](OTCPK:PCFNF), Platinum Group Metals’ [TSX:PTM] (PLG).

Nickel juniors

Talon Metals [TSX:TLO] (OTCPK:TLOFF) Tamarack - (JV with Rio Tinto)

Tamarack is a high grade nickel-copper-cobalt project located in Minnnesota, USA, with considerable exploration upside. Talon Metals owns a 51% project share, with potential to further earn-in to a 60% share by 2026.

No news for the month.

QPM Energy Limited [ASX:QPM](formerly Queensland Pacific Metals)

QPM is developing the 100% owned Townsville Energy Chemicals Hub ("TECH") battery metals refinery Project. QPM has secured binding offtake agreements for the sale of nickel and cobalt with LG Energy Solutions, POSCO and General Motors. They also have the revenue producing Moranbah Gas Project (“MGP”), which captures waste mine gas from operating coal mines as well as its own petroleum leases.

On July 30 QPM Energy announced:

June 2025 Quarterly Report...As at the date of this report, QPM’s cash and cash equivalents balance is $20.2m...Subsequent to the end of the quarter, QPM and the Queensland State Government executed an agreement relating to the $8m government grant for the TECH Project...QPM will be able to advance the TECH Project to a point where feasibility work is complete and attract interested parties that may arise from a change in nickel sentiment. During the quarter, QPM commenced high acid concentration leach testwork. Using higher acid concentration would reduce the amount of water in the processing circuit, which would lead to capital (less volume / lower scale) and operating (less energy required to evaporate water) cost reductions.

NexMetals Mining Corp. [TSXV:NEXM] (PRMLF) - formerly Premium Resources Ltd. [TSXV:PREM](NEXM)

NexMetals Mining has multi-metal projects in Canada (Post Creek and Halcyon) and Botswana (Selebi Main and Selebi North).

On June 30 NexMetals announced: "NexMetals drills 13.50 metres of 3.68% CuEq expanding mineralization significantly below Selebi North resource footprint."

On July 16 NexMetals announced: "NexMetals begins trading on the Nasdaq under the symbol NEXM."

On July 17 NexMetals announced:

NexMetals receives US$150 million Letter of Interest from the Export-Import Bank of the United States for its critical metals projects in Botswana...The LI indicates the potential for up to US $150 million in financing, with a maximum 15-year repayment tenor, to support the re-development of NEXM's Selebi and Selkirk nickel-copper-cobalt-platinum group metal mines in Botswana.

On July 28 NexMetals announced:

NexMetals achieves an initial 15.2% head grade increase at Selebi North using modern technology demonstrating high recoveries and significant waste reduction.

Canada Nickel Company [TSXV:CNC](OTCQX:CNIKF)

On July 7 Canada Nickel announced:

Continued exploration success at MacDiarmid, provides exploration update. Five holes testing central region at MacDiarmid yielded multi-hundred metre intervals including 0.25% nickel over 363 metres in hole MAC 25-09.

On July 15 Canada Nickel announced:

Initial Resources at Mann Central and Texmont...

Mann Central similar scale to Initial Crawford Nickel Project Resource:

  • Indicated Mineral Resource of 236.7 million tonnes grading 0.22% nickel containing 0.52 million tonnes of nickel.
  • Inferred Mineral Resource of 543.2 million tonnes grading 0.21% nickel containing 1.15 million tonnes of nickel. Exploration Target1 of an additional 0.6-2.0 billion tonnes grading 0.19 – 0.21% nickel.
  • Initial Texmont Measured & Indicated Mineral Resource of 37.8 million tonnes grading 0.29% nickel containing higher grade resource of 1.69 million tonnes at 0.71% nickel including 248,000 tonnes at 1.03% nickel.

Ardea Resources [ASX:ARL] (OTCPK:ARRRF)

In total, Ardea has 6.1Mt of contained nickel and 386,000t of contained cobalt. They own the KNP Project near Kalgoorlie in Western Australia and have a JV consortium agreement to earn into 50% share of the KNP – Goongarrie Hub with Sumitomo Metal Mining and Mitsubishi Corporation). Ardea is also exploring for gold and nickel sulphide on their >5,100 km2 of 100% controlled tenements in the Eastern Goldfields region of Western Australia.

On July 4 Ardea Resources announced:

Goongarrie Hub Project DFS reaches major milestone. Japanese Consortium earns 17.5% interest in KNPL as DFS expenditure reaches 50% milestone...The Consortium has also exercised its right to appoint a director to the KNPL board.

On July 30 Ardea Resources announced:

June 2025 QUARTERLY ACTIVITIES REPORT...As of 30 June 2025, Ardea remains in a strong financial position with $14.6M cash on hand and no debt...With $49M in committed funding remaining at the end of the Quarter and substantial progress made across key study areas, the Company is well positioned to complete all planned work associated with the ongoing Definitive Feasibility Study (DFS).

Centaurus Metals Limited [ASX:CTM] (OTCQX:CTTZF)

Centaurus Metals is an Australian-based minerals exploration company focused on the near-term development of the Jaguar Nickel Sulphide Project, in Northern Brazil.

On July 24 Centaurus Metals announced:

High grade copper-gold hits expand Boi Novo mineralisation...A new shallow copper-gold mineralised zone has been discovered at the Nelore East Prospect...

  • 1.5m at 2.84% Cu and 0.90g/t Au from 91.9m (BON-DD-25-040).
  • 6.8m at 1.89% Cu and 1.08g/t Au from 107.6m (BON-DD-25-040).
  • 10.2m at 0.68% Cu and 0.10g/t Au from 29.3m (BON-DD-25-041)...

On July 28 Centaurus Metals announced:

Quarterly Activities/Appendix 5B Cash Flow Report. JAGUAR NICKEL SULPHIDE PROJECT, BRAZIL. Outstanding outcomes from the Jaguar Value Engineering Process (JVEP)1:

  • JORC Ore Reserve estimate of 52Mt @ 0.78% Ni for 406,100 tonnes of contained nickel.
  • 15-year open pit mine life with low strip ratio of 4.9:1.
  • Average annual nickel production of 22,600tpa over first seven years of full production delivering free operating cash-flows over this period of US$169 million pa (A$264 million pa).
  • First quartile life-of-mine C1 cash cost of US$3.34/lb and AISC of US$4.43/lb Ni (payable basis).
  • Confirmed low capital intensity with pre-production capex of US$380 million.
  • Post-Tax life-of-mine operating cash flow of US$2.00 billion.
  • Post-Tax NPV 8 of A$1.15 billion and IRR of 34% pa.
  • Capital payback of 1.8 years from first nickel concentrate production.
  • Underground Resource demonstrates potential for significant mine life extension beyond the open pit operations.

Alliance Nickel [ASX:AXN] (formerly GME Resources)

On July 16 Alliance Nickel announced:

Quarterly Activities/Appendix 5B Cash Flow Report.

  • Alliance secures additional A$1.2 million in unsecured loan facilities from major shareholder Zeta Resources and Company Directors
  • Existing A$4 million in loans extended to 30 June 2026 with interest capitalised until loan maturity
  • Extension and additional funding at competitive terms signals lenders' strong commitment to Alliance's long-term objectives
  • Discussions with existing and potential partners continue in relation to potential further offtake and potential strategic investment.

Sama Resources [TSXV: SME] [GR;8RS] (OTCPK:SAMMF)

The Samapleu-Grata Nickel-Copper Project is a 60/40 joint venture between Ivanhoe Electric and Sama Resources.

No news for the month.

Murchison Minerals [TSXV:MUR] (OTCPK:MURMF)

On July 21 Murchison Minerals announced: "Murchison Minerals applies for extension of Price Protection for Private Placement...of a further 30 days."

Power Metallic Mines Inc. [TSXV:PNPN] (CMETF) - formerly Power Nickel

On July 10 Power Metallic Mines announced: "Update on Summer 2025 Exploration Programs at Nisk Project...Exploration is well underway for the summer to fall drilling campaign."

On July 14 Power Metallic Mines announced: "Power Metallic closes on Li-FT Power land acquisition...to acquire a 100% interest in 313 mineral claims totalling 167 km² from Li-FT Power Ltd."

On July 23 Power Metallic Mines announced: "Power Metallic demonstrates positive initial mineralogy results - PGE enrichment associated with chalcopyrite and cubanite."

Garibaldi Resources [TSXV:GGI] [GR:RQM] [LN:OUX6] (OTCPK:GGIFF)

On July 18 Garibaldi Resources announced: "Garibaldi announces closing of Private Placement...or gross proceeds of $1,008,139.16."

The Metals Company (TMC)

No news for the month; however there was an SEC filing stating: "Initial filing by director officer or owner of more than ten percent." Details here.

Other juniors

Alaska Energy Metals Corp. [TSXV:AEMC] (OTCQB:AKEMF), Artemis Resources [ASV:ARV], Australian Mines [ASX:AUZ], Azure Minerals [ASX:AZS] (OTCPK:AZRMF), Blackstone Minerals [ASX:BSX], Cassini Resources [ASX: CZI], Class 1 Nickel and Technologies Ltd. [CSE:NICO] (OTCQB:NICLF), Electra Battery Materials [TSXV:ELBM] (ELBMF), Electric Royalties [TSXV:ELEC], EV Nickel [TSXV:EVNI] (OTCPK:EVNIF), First Atlantic Nickel [TSXV:FAN] (OTCQB:FANCF), Flying Nickel Mining Corp. [TSXV:FLYN], FPX Nickel [TSXV:FPX], Grid Metals Corp [TSXV:GRDM], Go Metals [CSE:GOCO] (OTCPK:GOCOF), Huntsman Exploration [TSXV:HMAN] (OTCPK:BBBMF), Inomin Mines [TSXV:MINE], New Age Metals [TXV:NAM], Nickel Creek Platinum [TSX:NCP] (OTCQX:NCPCF), Nordic Nickel Limited [ASX:NNL], Pancontinental Resources Corporation [TSXV:PUC], Polymet Mining [TSX:POM], Renforth Resources [CSE:RFR] (OTCQB:RFHRF), Rox Resources [ASX:RXL], S2 Resources (ASX:S2R), Stillwater Critical Minerals [TSXV:PGE] (OTCQB:PGEZF), Sunrise Energy Metals [ASX:SRL] (OTCQX:SREMF), Surge Battery Metals Inc. [TSXV:NILI] [FRA:DJ5C] (OTCQX:NILIF), Talisman Mining Ltd. [ASX:TLM], Tartisan Nickel Corp. [CSE:TN] (OTCQB:TTSRF), Transition Metals [TSXV:XTM], URU Metals Ltd. [LSE:URU] [GR:NVRA], Wall Bridge Mining [TSX:WM], WIN Metals Ltd [ASX:WIN], and Zeb Nickel Corp. [TSXV:ZBNI] (OTCQB:ZBNIF).

Note: Some of the above companies are covered in the Cobalt monthly news.

Nickel miners ETF

Below is the Sprott Nickel Miners ETF. You can view the top holdings here.

Sprott Nickel Miners ETF (NIKL) - One year price chart - Price = US$11.90

Conclusion

Nickel spot prices were flat the last month.

Highlights for the month were:

  • US tariffs on many countries will hit on August 1. This has potential to impact the nickel market, noting there are some conflicting reports as to whether nickel as a critical mineral is exempted from US tariffs.
  • CEO Mark Selby says Canada supplies half of US nickel but can find new buyers if trade tensions escalate.
  • Nickel sulphate (mostly for EV batteries) demand shows notable weakness, as the trend towards LFP batteries grows.
  • Fastmarkets - Nickel oversupply is due more to strong production growth rather than to weak demand. Without greater supply discipline from Indonesia and China, which together account for 78% of global refined production, there is little scope for any near-term market rebalancing.
  • Nornickel - 1H 2025 consolidated nickel output decreased 4% YoY to 87 kt.
  • Vale's Q2 nickel production totaled 40.3 kt, 44% (12.4 kt) higher y/y.
  • Glencore announces US$1B 2025/26 share buyback program. Glencore H1 own sourced nickel production of 36.6kt, 7% YoY lower.
  • BHP Group nickel production in Q4, FY 2025 was 0.3kt, down 99% YoY.
  • Anglo American Q2 nickel production decreased to 9.5kt, down 5% YoY.
  • Sherritt's Q2 finished nickel production at the Moa Joint Venture was 3,431 tonnes. Q2 adjusted net loss from continuing operations was $25.6M or $(0.06) per share.
  • IGO Limited - Higher quarterly production and lower unit cash costs at Nova.
  • Sumitomo Metal Mining has repurchased 1,047,000 of its own shares.
  • Nickel Industries RKEF operations (80% share) Q2 nickel production was 30.463kt, down 4% QoQ.
  • QPM and the Queensland State Government executed an agreement relating to the $8m government grant for the TECH Project.
  • NexMetals receives US$150 million Letter of Interest from the Export-Import Bank of the United States for its critical metals projects in Botswana.
  • Canada Nickel drills multi-hundred metre intervals including 0.25% nickel over 363 metres at MacDiarmid. Canada Nickel Mann Central similar scale to Initial Crawford Nickel Project Resource.
  • Centaurus Metals high grade copper-gold hits expand Boi Novo mineralisation.
  • Power Metallic Mines closes on Li-FT Power land acquisition.

As usual all comments are welcome.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: Jerónimo Martins, SGPS, S.A. 2025 Q2 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation
data.description: The following slide deck was published by Jerónimo Martins, SGPS, S.A. in conjunction with their 2025 Q2 earnings call.

data.title: What Most Investors Are Missing: 3 Dividend Picks With Serious Potential Upside
data.description:
Introduction

I think almost everyone reading this has heard of contrarian investing.

To use the definition presented by Investopedia:

contrarian investing is an investment style in which investors purposefully go against prevailing market trends by selling when others are buying and buying when most investors are selling."

Essentially, it means doing the opposite of the crowd to generate superior returns.

When I started investing at the age of 15/16, I always followed the crowd in my first years, buying the most hyped stocks because it just felt good to buy something everyone agreed on. After all, if everyone agrees, it has to be good?

You can imagine what my returns looked like.

The tough thing about contrarian investing is finding opportunities. Asking the question "What's popular right now?" and simply doing the opposite of that is a fantastic way to ruin one's portfolio.

After all, if you had bet against popular investments, odds are you would have been short Big Tech for at least a decade and avoided the market in general.

Personally, I don't see myself as a contrarian investor.

However, I am someone who avoids the hottest picks and builds my positions around core ideas that the market may be missing.

For example, as I wrote in my recently released full portfolio overview:

  • I have a quarter of my entire net worth in LandBridge (LB) and Texas Pacific Land (TPL), two stocks that allow me to benefit from the massive water opportunity in the Permian Basin, commercial deals like data centers, and potentially higher oil and gas prices in the future.
  • I have invested roughly a fifth of my portfolio in aerospace and defense contractors that provide me with elevated secular growth, subdued cyclical risks, and track records of elevated dividend growth.
  • I prefer "picks and shovels" like Comfort Systems USA (FIX), which allows me to benefit from high data center demand by buying one of the few companies capable of delivering state-of-the-art HVAC systems and services.
  • I benefit from economic reshoring through advanced transportation companies.
  • Etc.

Essentially, I am not a contrarian, but someone who avoids the most popular investments.

That said, I still include contrarian aspects.

For example, the overwhelming majority of the capital I put to work this year was pushed into the market during the March/April sell-off when I remained bullish despite the escalating trade war.

It was a low-risk, high-reward decision.

  • If I were wrong and stocks had kept dropping, I would still have gotten my favorite companies at decent valuations.
  • Because I turned out to be right, I used a brief window of opportunity to buy in a market environment that is everything but cheap.

In general, it simply makes sense to buy when others are fearful. This includes buying when economic data is downright bad.

As we can see below, the average 12-month forward return of an investment made when consumer sentiment bottoms is 24.1%. An investment at sentiment peaks would have returned just 3.9%, on average.

This means two things:

  1. When sentiment is weak, we often get stocks below their fair value.
  2. The longer-term returns of the stock market are so good that even buying at unfavorable prices tends to pay off. In other words, even if you're not a market timer, just sticking to one's plan is often a great idea.

Right now, one of my theses is that the best value is found in cyclical value. It's something I have brought up in a number of articles, including an article titled "Buried Under Fear: One Of The Best Opportunities I've Seen In Years."

Here's a part of my takeaway:

Despite rising volatility, geopolitical risks, and stretched valuations, I'm leaning into cyclical dividend stocks, an area most investors are still avoiding.

That's because the early signs of improving conditions are showing up, and I believe the reward far outweighs the risk over the long haul.

Essentially, this isn't about being reckless, but about being prepared, forward-looking, and contrarian when the odds are in our favor.

As it turns out, this thesis remains well on track.

For example, the average of two of my leading indicators, the Empire State and the Philadelphia Fed manufacturing surveys, shows upside momentum.

This is a big deal, as these indicators are highly correlated to their bigger brother, the ISM Manufacturing Index, one of my all-time favorite indicators for cyclical growth expectations.

The chart below compares the ISM Manufacturing Index (purple line) to the ratio between industrial stocks (XLI) and the S&P 500.

With strong conviction, I can say that rising cyclical expectations indicate we can now buy alpha in industrial stocks, something we haven't been able to do since 2021, when cyclical growth peaked. Since then, mainly secular growth themes like economic reshoring and data centers have been driving the economy (broadly speaking).

Currently, this thesis is not getting a lot of love, as trade tensions have caused elevated recession risks.

However, with trade tensions dwindling after major deals like the one with the European Union have been closed, these risks are lower. Also, and this is way more important, I have written about the Trump administration's push for higher nominal growth, even if it comes at the cost of higher inflation.

I wrote this in an article (among others) titled "The Paradigm Shift Is Coming - Here's How To Prepare Your Portfolio."

Now, the Trump administration appears to be embracing that reality with a high-stakes gamble, as it is likely to target (or accept) 4% inflation and high single-digit nominal GDP growth.

While I may sound a bit dramatic, I believe this is a full-blown paradigm shift, one that could-and likely will-renew markets for years.

For investors, this means adapting to a world where cyclical stocks, hard assets, and value will likely outperform growth.

It also needs to be said that because the market's earnings yield is now equal to the yield on longer-term government bonds, we desperately need elevated earnings growth to keep stocks, in general, attractive. Given that the benefits of Big Tech leadership may not last, President Trump is almost required to do whatever is needed to kickstart cyclical growth.

This is more evidence of why I expect a lot more poly support for my thesis.

And, considering the implications for inflation, it's one of my most important theses right now.

That's also why I'm so committed to finding the right picks.

While the opportunities are truly endless, in the second part of this article, I'll share three of my favorite dividend (growth) picks for this thesis.

Moving forward, I'll provide much more food for thought.

3 Fantastic Picks to Consider Right Now

1. Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL)

The Old Dominion Freight Line is a company I brought up on X (formerly known as Twitter) the other day, calling it one of my favorites in cyclical value.

Although ODFL yields just 0.7%, it has a payout ratio of just 20%, a five-year dividend CAGR of 32%, negative net debt (more cash than gross debt), and the most efficient less-than-truckload ("LTL") network in all of North America.

While I usually dislike trucking, LTL is different, as it relies on a strong network of service centers that connect senders and receivers in the most efficient way.

Essentially, ODFL has figured out the most efficient way to collect goods, bring them to distribution/service centers, sort them, and then efficiently bring them to customers with minimal delays and damage.

As easy as this may sound, it's super hard to achieve.

However, ODFL has mastered it.

For 15 straight years, ODFL has won the #1 National Carrier award from Mastio, which is supported by its 99% on-time service ratio, its cargo claims ratio of a mere 0.1%, and the fact that it uses technology and service center density like no other company in its industry, resulting in a sub-80% operating ratio.

It also helps that it owns almost all of its 261 service centers, which provides it with prime real estate close to high-demand areas and very limited rent risks.

Thanks to these tailwinds, the company has grown its revenue by 10.3% per year from 2004 to 2024, without any major acquisitions and strong pricing power. As we can see below (using third-party data), the company has the best premium offering and an average price for its operations, meaning it has plenty of pricing power.

Essentially, the company has used these benefits to organically grow its market share from 2.9% in 2002 to 12.0% in 2023.

Given that LTL operators cater to industrial companies and enjoy secular tailwinds from economic reshoring, I expect a cyclical bottom to lead to tremendous capital gains in the years ahead.

The chart below shows the distance ODFL shares trade below their all-time high (red line) compared to the ISM Manufacturing Index. Ignoring its valuation, the odds seem to be aligned for a steep rebound.

If I had more cash, I would be an aggressive buyer of ODFL equity, as it's one of the most attractive stocks in my portfolio right now.

Moreover, while ODFL isn't "traditionally" cheap at 28x earnings, the company usually trades well above 25x due to its pricing power and growth potential.

As I expect current growth expectations of 15% and 11% EPS growth in 2026 and 2027, respectively, to be boosted substantially, I have little doubt that ODFL is bottoming, even if it wasn't able to convince investors that economic growth is improving in its 2Q25 earnings call, which sent its stock down more than 9%.

While ODFL remains volatile, I expect we are in a bottoming process for cyclical stocks, which could unlock tremendous value in the years ahead.

2. North American Construction Group (NOA)

For this one, you'll need a thick skin, meaning it's the wrong investment for the majority of conservative investors.

Yet, I'm still bringing it up, as the company is unknown by many and because I expect this Canadian company, with listings in Toronto and New York, to deliver substantial capital gains in the years ahead.

I also brought it up in a relatively recent article on dividend stocks that fly under the radar, which you can read here if you're interested.

Founded about 70 years ago, NACG/NOA is a major owner of heavy equipment. It owns this equipment because its business model is entirely focused on heavy equipment rentals and services, such as maintenance.

This is a smart business model, as many customers do not want to own heavy machinery, as it's expensive, hard to maintain, and because depreciation and other costs can make it hard to improve a company's margins.

Interestingly enough, NACG's customers are not random contractors but mostly mining companies. This includes Australian metal miners and Canadian oil giants, as oil is mined using trucks in Canada's famous oil sands.

For example, mining 110 thousand barrels of oil per day requires 100 400-ton trucks, almost ten 800-ton loaders, 18 600-HP dozers, and other equipment.

Although it has a market cap of less than $500 million, it has a number of competitive advantages I covered in my prior article as well:

  • Building a fleet of high-quality equipment is very expensive. While capital isn't a huge barrier for many, ordering equipment can take a long time, especially in times of elevated demand. Having a good relationship with a producer like Caterpillar is key.
  • Without a strong maintenance network, competing is impossible.
  • Operating in areas like Canada and Australia requires strong connections with indigenous partners and governments.
  • Having a strong safety track record can help onboard new business.
  • Operating in harsh conditions is different from providing equipment for random construction projects.

Including M&A, the company is now more aggressively focusing on opportunities in Canada's growing energy industry and Australia's super-fragmented rental market, two opportunities that have allowed the company to grow its EPS by 4.4x since 2018.

Going forward, it aims for 5% to 10% annual growth, with benefits from incremental project wins that do not require it to boost CapEx.

Moreover, currently yielding 2.2%, it has a payout ratio of a mere 13% and a five-year CAGR of 24%, meaning it has tremendous dividend growth potential.

Valuation-wise, the company trades at just 8.2x earnings. Even at 11.5x earnings, there is room for a theoretical total return of nearly 80% over the next few years, which I expect to be higher if I'm right about a bottom in cyclical growth.

But then again, please be aware that NOA shares are super volatile.

While it could become a homerun investment if I'm right, I don't own it because I already own so many cyclical stocks.

Also, I'm out of cash for the time being.

The next pick is a high-yielding investment for the income-focused readers among you.

3. Energy Transfer (ET)

Energy Transfer is a Master Limited Partnership, which means it issues a K-1 form. It also means its shares are called "units" and its dividends are called "distributions."

The company is the backbone of America's energy industry, as it owns more than 130,000 miles of pipelines, fathering capacity of roughly 20.4 million MMBtu/d of natural gas and 1.1 million barrels of natural gas liquids.

It also owns fractionation and transportation assets that service every part of the energy value chain, including transmission lines, processing facilities, barges, trucks, and other solutions to get fossil fuel products to customers.

With that said, despite being an energy company, its operations are rather anti-cyclical, as more than 90% of its EBITDA comes from fee-based contracts.

Despite that, its unit price has suffered a bit, as it trades roughly $4-5 below its 52-week high, triggered by the decline in oil and natural gas prices.

While these headwinds do not hurt its bottom line, the market tends to throw the baby out with the bathwater to incorporate potential risks like a scenario where energy prices are so low that producers are forced to reduce output.

If this lasts, it could hurt revenue growth.

However, I think these risks are massively overblown. I also believe a cyclical upswing would cause substantially higher revenue growth in the next few years, as major buyers like China and other manufacturing nations would step up purchases of natural gas liquids, crude oil, liquified natural gas, and more.

Energy Transfer is in a great spot to benefit from this, as it has a wide range of growth projects, including Permian processing plant upgrades, the Sabina 2 pipeline conversion, its Nederland Flexport NGL expansion, the Badger processing plant, and the mighty Gateway NGL Pipeline.

On top of that, it offers data centers to be built directly on its network. As utilities are increasingly overwhelmed by high power demand, I expect solutions like these to surface a lot.

As ET already serves power plants, with access to low-cost natural gas in the Permian, it's a prime winner for deals with high volume growth potential.

The best thing about ET is that while investors wait for a growth rebound, they get to enjoy its 7.5% yield. Although the distribution was cut during the pandemic to protect the balance sheet, the company is now on a much more sustainable path, which leads me to believe its distribution is now bulletproof, meaning not even a steep recession will force the company to cut.

I also expect the distribution to grow in the mid-single-digit range every year on a prolonged basis.

Takeaway

Contrarian investing isn't about blindly betting against the crowd.

It's about spotting opportunities where the market's fear or neglect creates value.

As I explained in this article, I'm not a strict contrarian, which may not come as a shock to many readers. However, I avoid overhyped trends and focus instead on overlooked sectors with strong fundamentals.

Right now, that means leaning into cyclical value, where early signs of recovery (like improving manufacturing data) suggest big potential upside.

Today, we covered three opportunities that cater to various types of investors:

  • Old Dominion Freight Line: This company is a best-in-class LTL operator with pricing power and tremendous secular and cyclical growth opportunities.
  • North American Construction Group: The smallest company in this article is a volatile but high-value play on heavy equipment rentals, which is trading at a dirt-cheap valuation.
  • Energy Transfer: This is a mission-critical, resilient midstream company with a 7.5% yield, positioned to capitalize on energy demand and data center growth.

All things considered, the key to generating wealth is patience, selectivity, and buying when others are hesitant.

Right now, one of the best ways to do that is by diving into cyclical value.

Risks to My Thesis

The biggest risk to my thesis is a recession or simply the failure of the Trump administration to kickstart cyclical growth. It would likely keep a lid on both ODFL and NOA, while ET has an additional safety layer that could keep it attractive even in an environment of subdued growth and unattractive risk premia for stocks, in general.

data.title: Amazon: Is The Post-Earnings Slump Justified?
data.description:
Article Thesis

Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) reported strong quarterly earnings results on Thursday. While there are macro uncertainties, growth momentum looks healthy -- despite a valuation that is far from low, I nevertheless think that other Magnificent 7 stocks are more attractive compared to Amazon.

Past Coverage

I have written about Amazon.com here on Seeking Alpha in the past, most recently one quarter ago when I covered AMZN's Q1 earnings. With the company having reported its Q2 results on Thursday afternoon, and with one quarter having passed since my last article on AMZN, it is time to take another look at the company today.

What Happened?

Following the market's close, Amazon.com reported its Q2 earnings results on Thursday afternoon. These were the headline numbers from AMZN's report:

Amazon.com beat estimates on both lines, which naturally is good news. The revenue beat was sizeable, at around 3%, while the earnings per share beat was very large, at around 20%. The fact that Amazon outperformed expectations wasn't very surprising, though, as the company had done the same in the preceding three quarters -- AMZN thus has a history of performing better than what Wall Street forecasts.

The market nevertheless reacted negatively to AMZN's Q2 report, with shares trading down by around 8% at the time of writing. Q2 results were better than forecasted by the analyst community on a company-wide basis, thus the harsh market reaction is surprising at first sight. The post-earnings sell-off could be the result of profit taking, or investors were unhappy with AMZN's guidance for the current quarter, or they were unhappy about the relatively weak profit growth rate of AWS -- more on that later.

Amazon: Much Better Profitability Compared To Expectations

Let's delve into the company's Q2 results: Amazon's revenue growth of 13% was quite appealing in relative terms, although not as good as what Magnificent 7 peers Microsoft (MSFT) and Meta Platforms (META) reported on Wednesday. But AMZN's growth was more or less in line with that of Alphabet (GOOG)(GOOGL), and was better compared to Apple's (AAPL) growth of around 10%, and, of course, better than Tesla's (TSLA) negative growth rate. Relative to its big tech peers, Amazon thus performed reasonably well, but not great.

Revenue growth was possible thanks to growth across the company's different units. Amazon's North America segment, its biggest unit by far, generated revenue growth of 11%, which is very solid, and which lifted the quarterly total to more than $100 billion -- this unit alone is a $400+ billion a year juggernaut. In this unit, profit growth was quite strong, with operating profits rising by around 50% compared to one year earlier. The fact that profits grew so much more compared to the unit's revenues can be explained by operating leverage -- non-proportional costs do not climb when the company's business expands -- as well as by positive factors such as lower fuel expenses, thanks to relatively low oil prices in 2025 so far.

Amazon's International segment, which is around one-third the size of the North America business, saw pretty strong revenue growth of 16% compared to one year earlier, although that growth rate was impacted by currency rate movements to a significant degree: the US Dollar weakened against many other currencies during the second quarter, such as the Euro, which boosted the reported (in US Dollars) growth rate of the International unit in Q2. Adjusted for the currency impact, the International business grew by 11% year-over-year, which is weaker but still very healthy. Here, like in the North America business, profits grew at a nice pace, with operating leverage, lower fuel costs, etc. being the relevant factors for the margin growth the unit experienced relative to one year earlier.

Amazon's AWS, its highest-growth and arguably its most important unit, saw its revenues expand from $26.3 billion to $30.9 billion, which made for an increase of 17.5% over the last year. While the unit's relative growth rate has been stronger in the past, we have to consider that maintaining a very high-growth rate becomes more difficult over time, as the absolute size rises. Since AWS now generates more than $120 billion per year in (high-margin) revenue, it has to be expected that the unit won't grow by 20%-30% or even more per year anymore. That being said, Alphabet's Google Cloud grew by 32% in the most recent quarter, which is why some investors may have been a bit underwhelmed by the considerably weaker growth rate of AWS.

Looking at the profitability of AWS, things didn't look great: profits grew by around 10%, thus the unit's margin declined, and AWS saw considerably weaker profit growth than revenue growth. This is not only a major difference compared to Alphabet's Google Cloud, where margins expanded, but it is also a bit of a deviation compared to the historical trend -- generally, AWS saw stable or expanding margins. One can argue that if margin headwinds here were caused by growth investments, then that could pay off in the future, but the relatively weak profit growth rate of AWS nevertheless is a negative that investors should keep an eye on in future quarters, I believe.

Looking at cash flows, Amazon did reasonably well when it comes to generating cash from its operations: operating cash flow totaled a little more than $120 billion over the last year, up 12% compared to the result from a year ago. But capital expenditures were up quite a lot over the last year, which is why Amazon's free cash flows declined over the last year -- they stood at just $18 billion over the last four quarters combined, versus more than $50 billion one year earlier. For a company that is valued at around $2.4 billion, a $18 billion free cash flow result per year is not a lot -- that makes for a trailing twelve-month free cash flow multiple of around 130. The increased capital expenditures are, not surprisingly, the result of Amazon's AI investments. Other Magnificent 7 peers invest more and more cash into AI as well, such as META and GOOG; thus, this is not an Amazon-specific issue. But there is still some uncertainty here when it comes to when/if those heavy investments will pay off.

Amazon's Outlook

The retail business did very well in North America and internationally, and margin improvements here were quite positive. Momentum thus seems to be on Amazon's side right now.

Amazon's guidance for the current quarter is solid -- the company forecasts operating profits of around $18 billion, slightly ahead of the consensus estimate, and expects revenue growth in the 10% to 13% range, which is comparable to the Q2 growth rate or a little below that. Since AMZN tends to be conservative with its guidance, I would not be surprised to see revenue growth come in at the higher end of the guidance, which would mean a growth rate in line with Q2.

That being said, there are some macro risks -- both from tariffs and trade conflicts as well as from a potential recession. While the strong Q2 GDP print in the US makes a recession less likely in the near term, a recession down the road, caused by trade tensions, high-interest rates, etc., is not impossible. Amazon, as a consumer-focused company, would likely suffer should a recession occur.

Is Amazon An Attractive Investment?

Amazon grows at a nice pace in absolute terms, and at a solid, although not great, pace relative to other big tech companies. Its valuation, at around 37x forward earnings, is higher compared to Alphabet, Meta Platforms, Apple, and Microsoft. This makes me believe that Amazon is not among the most attractive big tech names to invest in right now -- Microsoft and Meta Platforms offer better growth at a lower valuation, while Alphabet offers comparable growth at a way lower valuation.

I do not think that Amazon is extremely overvalued, however, which is why I don't consider it a sell -- it's solid, but there are alternatives that are more attractive right now.

data.title: Median Household Income In June 2025
data.description:
Motio Research's initial estimate of U.S. median household income in June 2025 is $83,680. This figure is $530 (0.6%) higher than the firm's initial estimate of median household income of $83,150 in May 2025.

The firm's estimates are based on income data collected by the U.S. Census Bureau through its monthly Current Population Survey. This survey is conducted during the week containing the 12th day of the month following the month in which its data applies. Motio Research then adjusts its monthly estimates to account for the effects of seasonality and inflation, presenting its results in the form of an index with the median household income of January 2010 assigned a value of 100. The initial value of the firm's U.S. Real Median Household Income Index for June 2025 is 117.3.

The following screenshot of Motio Research's interactive chart shows how this index has changed from January 2010 through May 2025:

June 2025's increase in Motio Research's U.S. Real Median Household Income Index is the first significant improvement it has recorded since it last peaked in September 2024. Prior to June 2025, the index had been in a slowly falling trend.

Analyst's Notes

Political Calculations initial estimate of median household income in June 2025 based upon our alternate methodology is $83,641, which is $196 (or 0.2%) higher than our initial May 2025 estimate of $83,445. Our median household income estimate is $39 (or about 0.1%) lower than Motio Research's June 2025 estimate.

The latest update to Political Calculations' chart tracking Median Household Income in the 21st Century shows the nominal (red) and inflation-adjusted (blue) trends for median household income in the United States from January 2000 through June 2025. The inflation-adjusted figures are presented in terms of constant June 2025 U.S. dollars and are not seasonally adjusted, unlike the data used to produce Motio Research's Household Income index:

Political Calculations' monthly median household income estimates are derived from the Bureau of Economic Analysis' monthly aggregate wage and salary estimates for the U.S. population. In June 2025, the BEA's data underwent very small downward revisions for April (-0.006%) and May 2025 (-0.052%).

The rate at which median household income is increasing has slowed in nominal, noninflation-adjusted terms since December 2024 and in real, inflation-adjusted terms since October 2024, with the inflation-adjusted growth rate of median household income nearing stall speed. Both these developments indicate U.S. economic growth is slowing.

References

U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. Table 2.6. Personal Income and Its Disposition, Monthly, Personal Income and Outlays, Not Seasonally Adjusted, Monthly, Middle of Month. Population. [Online Database (via Federal Reserve Economic Data)]. Last Updated: 31 July 2025. Accessed: 31 July 2025.

U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. Table 2.6. Personal Income and Its Disposition, Monthly, Personal Income and Outlays, Not Seasonally Adjusted, Monthly, Middle of Month. Compensation of Employees, Received: Wage and Salary Disbursements. [Online Database (via Federal Reserve Economic Data)]. Last Updated: 31 July 2025. Accessed: 31 July 2025.

U.S. Department of Labor Bureau of Labor Statistics. Consumer Price Index, All Urban Consumers - (CPI-U), U.S. City Average, All Items, 1982-84=100. Not seasonally adjusted. [Online Database (via Federal Reserve Economic Data)]. Last Updated: 15 July 2025. Accessed: 15 July 2025.

Original Post

Editor's Note: The summary bullets for this article were chosen by Seeking Alpha editors.

data.title: Xcel Energy: Buy This Dividend Aristocrat In The Making
data.description:
This article was coproduced with Kody Kester.

We highly value companies that value shareholders. Please allow me (Kody) to explain further.

For newer readers and as a reminder to more established readers, we like to see the following attributes from the companies we highlight:

  1. Tangible growth catalysts. Businesses with clear tailwinds can't help but generally grow their earnings, which makes them more valuable over time.
  2. The balance sheet must also be investment-grade. This is because an investment-grade balance sheet affords a company a lower cost of capital. That can further support a company's growth plans.
  3. The business must have a reputation for consistent dividend growth and a manageable payout ratio. This shows a track record of prioritizing shareholders. Combined with the other characteristics, that often translates into ever-growing dividends.

We believe Xcel Energy (XEL) fulfills these requirements. When we last covered XEL with a buy rating a few months ago, we liked that it had matched or topped earnings guidance for 20 consecutive years. The company's five-year capital spending plan was encouraging. Its financial health was fine, too. XEL was also on pace to become a Dividend Aristocrat in a few more years. Shares were also undervalued by a double-digit percentage to our fair value estimate.

Fast forward to today, and the story remains much the same, with a bit more reason for optimism. XEL posted robust Q2 results. The company also upped its additional investment pipeline from $10 billion-plus to $15 billion-plus. XEL's balance sheet remains intact. The stock is still moderately discounted as well.

Xcel Energy Delivered A Strong Q2

This week, XEL shared its earnings report for the second quarter ended June 30th, 2025. The company's operating revenue rose by 8.6% over the year-ago period to $3.29 billion in the quarter. That came in ahead of the analyst consensus by $80 million.

What was behind XEL's top line growth during the second quarter?

As was also the case in the prior quarter, the regulated utility benefited from customer growth and higher usage for the second quarter. The former varied from 0.7% for SPS to 1.3% for PSCo in the quarter. The latter ranged from 0.2% for NSP-Minnesota to as much as 5% for SPS during the quarter.

XEL's business model also accounted for some of its operating revenue growth for the second quarter. This is because its regulated utility structure allowed it to pass higher electric fuel/purchased power and natural gas costs onto customers in the quarter.

XEL's ongoing diluted EPS soared 38.9% year-over-year to $0.75 during the second quarter. That exceeded the analyst consensus for the quarter by $0.11. Fuel cost recovery, sales and demand, and non-fuel riders for electric revenues, allowance for funds used during construction debt and equity, and natural gas revenues lifted ongoing diluted EPS. That more than neutralized greater electric fuel and purchased power costs, interest charges, natural gas costs, and depreciation and amortization.

Looking out over the next several years, the outlook for XEL has arguably become even better.

When we last covered it, the company had a $45 billion five-year capital spending plan for 2025 through 2029. That base capital plan alone was expected to generate a 9.4% annual growth rate in the rate base. The base capital plan remains unchanged.

However, XEL's forecast for additional capital investment (mostly over that period) is up from the prior figure of $10 billion to $15 billion now. In his opening remarks during the Q2 2025 earnings call, Chairman and CEO Bob Frenzel outlined several drivers of this incremental need.

XEL plans to invest an incremental $3 billion to $4 billion in regional transmission projects to support reliability and regional growth. The company also secured approval for two firm dispatchment projects totaling 720 megawatts and at least an additional 2,800 megawatts of company-owned wind in Minnesota.

As a reminder, XEL anticipates that every $1 billion of incremental capex would grow its rate base by around 25 basis points.

So, it's not hard to see how the company could deliver on the high end or even beat its 6% to 8% annual ongoing diluted EPS growth target. Analysts as a whole tend to agree with this assessment.

The FAST Graphs analyst consensus is that ongoing diluted EPS will rise by 9.1% in 2025 to $3.82. An additional 8.1% jump in ongoing diluted EPS to $4.13 is expected for 2026. In 2027, another 8% increase in ongoing diluted EPS to $4.46 is the current consensus.

XEL is also in an enviable financial position. The company anticipates that its FFO-to-debt ratio will hold at around 17% through at least 2029. This is enough to warrant Baa1 (BBB+ equivalent), BBB, and BBB+ credit ratings from Moody's, S&P, and Fitch, respectively (unless otherwise sourced or hyperlinked, all info in this subhead was according to XEL's Q2 2025 Earnings Press Release and XEL's Q2 2025 Earnings Presentation).

Fair Value Has Surpassed $80 A Share

Since May, XEL has returned 5% as the S&P 500 index (SP500) returned 14% during that time. Still, XEL has matched the S&P so far this year, posting a roughly 10% total return.

We believe that the regulated utility isn't finished rallying, either. Shares are trading at a forward 12-month P/E ratio of 18.4. According to data from FAST Graphs, that's moderately below the 10-year average P/E ratio of 20.4.

We also think that a reasonable fair value P/E ratio for XEL remains the 10-year average of 20.4. This is because XEL's 8.1% annual forward ongoing diluted EPS growth consensus remains considerably higher than the 10-year average of around 6%. In addition to our analysis of the fundamentals earlier, this builds a convincing case that the regulated utility's growth picture has never been stronger.

We would continue to argue that this offsets the valuation headwind that would otherwise arise from higher 10-year U.S. Treasury (US10Y) yields. Historically, XEL's earnings yield has offered a 200 to 250 basis point spread to the 10-year yield.

Moving forward, we believe that this could narrow to 100 to 150 basis points (it already has in recent years, which reflects the improved growth profile). Plugging that in with our assumption of a 10-year yield in the high 3% range, this is how we come out to our fair value P/E ratio of 20.4.

Currently, the calendar year 2025 is 58% complete. That leaves another 42% of 2025 and 58% of 2026 yet to come over the next 12 months. This yields a forward 12-month ongoing diluted EPS input of $4.

Applying our fair value multiple of 20.4, we compute a fair value of $82 a share. Against the current $74 share price (as of July 31, 2025), this works out to be a 10% discount to fair value.

If XEL matches the growth consensus and returns to fair value, it could generate a 14% total return by the end of June 2026. Through 2030, the annual total return potential would be 11% in such a case.

The Dividend Has More Room To Run

XEL's 3.1% forward dividend yield clocks in a bit below the utility sector's 3.4% forward median yield. That accounts for C grades from Seeking Alpha's Quant System for forward dividend yield and overall dividend yield, respectively.

From a dividend consistency and safety perspective, XEL is arguably a standout dividend stock.

The company has upped its dividend for 22 consecutive years. This easily bests the sector median of just 11.2 years. That's sufficient for the Quant System to award an A grade for overall dividend consistency.

There's also no indication that the company will stop growing the payout anytime soon. This is evidenced by the fact that XEL's ongoing diluted EPS payout ratio is set to be around 60% in 2025. For more perspective, that's less than the 75% payout ratio that rating agencies like to see from the industry, per The Dividend Kings' Zen Research Terminal. This explains the B- grade for overall dividend safety from the Quant System.

On the dividend growth front, XEL is respectable as well. The Quant System anticipates 4.9% annual forward dividend growth. That would be comparable to the sector median of 5.2%. Given that XEL has no shortage of investment opportunities and the payout ratio is on the high end of its targeted range, we think this is realistic. This is why the Quant System issues a C grade for the forward dividend per share growth metric.

Risks To Consider

XEL's catalysts remain in place to drive vigorous growth in the future. However, the regulated utility still has risks that could materially alter the investment thesis.

As we outlined in our prior article, the biggest risk to XEL arguably remains the concentration of its rate base in Colorado and Minnesota. We don't have the latest data from the upcoming 10-Q filing on the proportion of its rate base in these two geographies (NSP-Minnesota does include some customers in the Dakotas, too). Overall, we'd expect that these markets still comprise around three-quarters of its total rate base, though.

Thus, XEL's fundamentals largely hinge on Colorado (the larger rate base of the two) and Minnesota. Any adverse rate case outcomes in those states could weaken the company's financial metrics and diminish the risk profile.

That could warrant credit rating downgrades from the big rating agencies. This would likely interfere with XEL's ability to deliver on its growth ambitions.

Additionally, the company faces pronounced wildfire risks in Texas and Colorado. A report from Verisk Wildfire Analytics ranks these two states as the second and third most dangerous states for wildfires, behind only California.

XEL's implementation of wildfire mitigation plans in these states should help to somewhat balance out the risk. If any severe wildfires happen, however, the company could be on the hook for legal liabilities.

One final risk to XEL is the potential for a major cyber breach. The company has been a target of attempted hacks. If any are successful, that could jeopardize sensitive customer information. This could also lead to litigation being brought against XEL.

Summary: Xcel Energy Is A Buyable SWAN Stock

We remain bullish on XEL. The company had a solid second quarter. It also significantly upped its incremental investment opportunities forecast over the next five years. That provides it with plenty of ways to generate growth for the foreseeable future. XEL remains financially sound, too.

Finally, the stock is trading at a valuation that's less than our fair value estimate. This gives it decent upside in the near-term and the medium term. Thus, we're reiterating our buy rating.

data.title: Wall Street Breakfast Podcast: Trump Signs New Tariff Order
data.description:
Listen below or on the go on Apple Podcasts and Spotify

Trump issues revised tariff orders. (00:21) Reddit's (RDDT) results topper, robust forecast drive shares higher. (01:49) Palantir (PLTR) lands enterprise deal worth up to $10B from U.S. Army. (02:58)

This is an abridged transcript.

President Trump on Thursday signed an executive order revising “reciprocal” tariffs on dozens of countries, setting new duty rates between 10% and 41%.

According to the White House, any goods deemed to be transshipped to evade these duties will incur an additional 40% tariff.

Syria faces the highest tariff at 41%. Switzerland and South Africa will see rates of 39% and 30%, respectively, while shipments from Taiwan will face a 20% tariff, lower than the 32% set earlier.

Trump also proceeded with his plan to raise tariffs on Canadian imports to 35% from 25%, effective August 1, 2025.

We have a detailed A-Z list of countries and territories with their respective reciprocal tariffs in this article in the show notes section of the show.

The revised tariff rates will take effect for goods “entered for consumption on or after 12:01 a.m. Eastern Daylight Time, seven days after the order’s issuance,” unless otherwise specified. That would make it August 7th.

Countries not included in the latest order will be subject to a 10% duty, according to the directive. The updated order revises tariffs set under the earlier executive order from April.

China faces an August 12 deadline to finalize a lasting tariff agreement with the Trump administration, following preliminary deals struck in May and June.

Reddit (NYSE:RDDT) is up 15% in premarket action.

The social media company provided a strong forecast and reported a double beat for its second-quarter earnings.

For the third quarter, Reddit expects revenue between $535M and $545M and adjusted EBITDA between $185M-$195M. These numbers surpass the Bloomberg consensus estimate of $472.7M for revenue and $159.5M for adj. EBITDA.

In Q2, Reddit saw daily active unique users rise 21% from last year to 110.4M, ahead of the 109.75M estimate.

Net income for the three months ended June 30 was $89M, compared with a loss of $10M a year ago.

On a per-share basis, the California company earned 45 cents, ahead of the average analyst estimate of 19 cents.

Total revenue surged nearly 78% to $500M beating the consensus estimate. Advertising revenue was up 84% at $465M; revenue from other sources was $35M, up 24%.

Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR) has secured a major deal from the U.S. Army worth up to $10B over the next decade, which establishes a framework for the Army's future software and data needs.

The new enterprise agreement consolidates 75 contracts (15 prime contracts and 60 related contracts) into a single one. It’ll enhance military readiness, drive operational efficiency, and deliver cost efficiencies.

The deal establishes volume-based discounts for the contract's performance period of up to 10 years. The Army and other Department of Defense agencies have the option to buy Palantir's (NASDAQ:PLTR) commercial products during that period, not to exceed the $10B cap.

The Washington Post reported, the award is one of the biggest DoD contracts ever, and is the largest ever awarded to Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR).

What’s Trending on Seeking Alpha:

Amazon posts a powerhouse quarter again; shares fall as guidance is weighed

Apple rises after 'solid' Q3, as Wedbush says iPhone China-beat 'star of the show'

Trump issues 60-day ultimatum for big pharma to lower drug prices

Dow, S&P and Nasdaq futures are in the red. Crude oil is down 0.8% at $68/barrel. Bitcoin is down 0.5% at $115,000. Gold is up 0.1% at $3,294.

The FTSE 100 is down 0.5% and the DAX is down 1.7%.

The biggest movers for the day premarket: Figma (NYSE:FIG) +11% – Shares extended gains after a stunning NYSE debut on Thursday, where they soared 250%. The design software firm priced its IPO at $33 per share which is now worth $115.50.

On today’s economic calendar:

  • 8:30 am Employment Situation

  • 10:00 am ISM Manufacturing Index

data.title: Builders FirstSource: Q2 Results Point To Further Weakness (Rating Downgrade)
data.description:
Shares of Builders FirstSource (BLDR) have been a very poor performer over the past year, losing about a quarter of their value as the company has been hurt by weakening residential construction. Shares were flattish Thursday as investors digested weak quarterly earnings, suggesting that housing has yet to hit bottom. Even with this fall, shares are actually still up 15% since I last covered them in May. At that time, I rated BLDR a “hold,” and the stock’s performance has matched the market. With updated financials, now is a good time to see if a hold rating remains appropriate. I am more bearish.

In the company’s second quarter, Builders FirstSource earned $1.66, which was $0.26 below expectations as revenue declined to $4.2 billion, about $80 million lower than consensus. Adjusted EPS was $2.38, which was still down 32% from last year. BLDR is facing weaker demand from homebuilders given a softening housing market, and resultant excess capacity is leading to further margin pressure.

In Q2, gross margins contracted by 210bps to 30.7% resulting in an 11% drop in gross profit to $1.3 billion. Margins were up 20bps sequentially, but based on guidance, this will be short-lived with further margin compression looming. Despite declining revenue, SG&A grew by 0.5% to $818 million, as aggressive M&A has increased its operating footprint and increased costs. As a result of this, EBITDA declined by 24% to $506 million with margins at 12%, and free cash flow was down 31% to $255 million.

Over the past few years, BLDR has used windfall profits to diversify its business away from commodity lumber exposure, which does make strategic sense to me as that should over time reduce the volatility of earnings and give BLDR more control over its P&L. Value-added products accounted for 47% of sales, up 2% from 4 four years ago, as a result of this effort.

However, it is not having the desired effect. Value-added organic sales were down by 11% given lower housing starts while lumber was down 6%. Including M&A, manufactured products declined by 10% to $953 million while windows and doors fell by 8% to $1.03 billion. In other words, the commodity-centric part of its business is actually holding in better, even as lumber pricing was down 1.5%, a $34 million headwind.

This is because even its value-added products are exposed to home construction activity. As you can see, the single-family construction market accounts for the lion’s share of BLDR’s business. I would also note that while multifamily is a small part of the business it will still drag $500 million on sales this year, though most of this occurred in H1 as multifamily began slowing more aggressively during 2024.

The challenge BLDR faces is that fewer homes are under construction. With declining prices across the Sun Belt, builders are pulling back. While lumber demand is high shortly after construction commences, value-added products like doors and windows are generally installed a bit later in the construction process. This is why the ongoing decline in the number of homes being built is beginning to hits its value-added segment harder. This trend is also well entrenched, and while the speed of the decline is uncertain, it seems highly likely that fewer homes will be under construction in 3, 6, and 12 months from today. In other words, the business has not hit bottom yet.

Indeed, as you can see below, housing starts have fallen sharply this year. With starts running lower, that means fewer homes will be entering the “under construction” phase. This will be a headwind for all aspects of the business. With mortgage rates unlikely to fall sharply, I struggle to see a significant turnaround in homebuying activity given challenging affordability levels. As such, we are likely to see builders continue to pull back on new supply until the market finds a clearer equilibrium.

Interestingly, on the earnings call, management said the housing market is worse than homebuilders have acknowledged on their earnings calls and in public statements. Now, BLDR does operate significantly in the markets that are facing the most pressure like Florida, Texas, and Arizona, and that may also be contributing to BLDR’s pessimistic take. Management is now forecasting single-family starts to be down 10-12%. It previously expected housing starts to be down mid-single digits.

Because of this, management slashed guidance with the high end of sales now $450 million below the previous low-end. Similarly, EBITDA sits below the entire previous range. Management only trimmed the top-end of its free cash flow guidance, given it is also slashing cap-ex to preserve cash flow.

It is also important to note that while Builders FirstSource’s balance sheet used to be a source of strength, that no longer is the case. While BLDR still describes leverage as “low,” it is above its 1-2x target, and with EBITDA continuing to decline from last year, this metric will continue to rise through year end. Importantly, it does not have maturity before 2030. In other words, its balance sheet is not strong but is not a real problem either.

Given the market downturn, I expect that BLDR will be comfortable running leverage a bit above target, and I still view sub-2.5x as reasonable. While the company did $391 million of share repurchases, these were largely in April. With leverage more likely to approach 3x, I expect a significant slowdown in buybacks in H2. It is possible that BLDR continues to halt repurchases, and M&A remains a higher capital priority, though in this environment, I believe BLDR should be cautious about over-expanding. Due to buybacks, there has been a 7.5% share count reduction over the past year.

Back in May, I saw the company generating $8-$8.25 in free cash flow and felt that the stock would be worth $130 if the US avoided a recession. As recession fears have faded, that is indeed where the stock traded at. At the midpoint of guidance, this company has about $8-$8.10 of free cash flow capacity. In other words, revised guidance really just brings BLDR’s view in line with my own existing expectations.

I am cautious that we may see further degradation in housing and expect BLDR to come in at the lower half of guidance, or $7.80-$8.10/share of free cash flow. I am more concerned that the pace of recovery will be substantially slower. The current number of houses under construction essentially supports near-term revenue, but with mortgage rates likely stuck in the 6.5-7.25% into next year in my view, I struggle to see housing construction bottoming within the next 12 months. Plus, with a weaker balance sheet, the amount of free cash flow it can return to investors next year is likely to narrow.

At $125, shares now have a roughly ~6.3% free cash flow, broadly consistent with my prior fair value estimate. However, I do not see free cash rising until 2027 at the soonest, and I believe investors may be disappointed with the pace of recovery and 2026 capital returns, especially when many homebuilders have better balance sheets and ~10% capital return capacity. As such, I see shares resuming their fall and pushing back to the $100-110 area, or closer to and 8% free cash flow, reflecting a weaker balance and slower recovery. With shares up 15% since May, now is a time for investors to exit BLDR. I view BLDR as a "sell."
